surface: docs/architecture.md CONTRIBUTING.md install.sh scripts/install scripts/validate.sh scripts/validation scripts/eval-teamwork.py scripts/teamwork_tooling scripts/tests scripts/grill_contract.py scripts/test_live_eval_runner.py scripts/run-installed-teamwork-live-eval.py evals/teamwork
decision: accept the canonical tree contract, coarse producer modularization, and hardened opt-in installed-Codex semantic canary
reason: Before, canonical and generated surfaces were implicit, three public producer entrypoints contained about 4,800 lines of mixed responsibilities, and installed-package live output had no manifest-bound scored review path. After, one tracked architecture map defines ownership, stable public commands delegate to cohesive modules without freezing accidental namespaces, and a bounded Codex lane separates installed inventory, execution, structural status, model provenance, and external semantic verdict. Future canary subprocesses use a Git-root preflight, an allowlisted environment, fail-visible cleanup, and fixed-schema redaction. The retained treatment remains availability-only and does not prove automatic activation, resolved model, ambient-variable absence, broad reliability, or cross-platform parity.
